comment: Single owner of release identity, mathematical claim status, and the machine-readable paper map. Human surfaces (README, paper, SCOPE.md, CITATION.cff) project from this file; Lean remains proof authority, and scripts/check_release.py fails the build when a checked projection or graph edge drifts.

machine_readable_paper.purpose: Give humans and agent harnesses one bounded map from the open problems and paper argument to claim status, Lean declarations, module dependencies, validation, and explicit non-claims.
authority_order: Lean source checked by the pinned Lean kernel
docs/claims.json for release identity, status taxonomy, coordinates, and navigation relationships
the authored exposition for mathematical explanation
README.md, SCOPE.md, docs/SOURCE_MAP.md, and docs/WAVE_INDEX.md as reader projections
machine_readable_paper.projection_boundary: The module graph records exact internal Lean imports. The argument graph is an authored navigation map, not an additional proof object and not a novelty or priority claim.

entrypoints.intent: Understand what this repository proves, reduces, cites, and leaves open.
Trace one mathematical claim from exposition to checked source.
See how the #249 reduction, #257 families, carry trunk, and open targets relate.
Modify a formal or editorial surface without allowing status or source coordinates to drift.

projections.role: Generated index of every public Lean declaration, source coordinate, signature excerpt, module import edge, generated-certificate marker, and principal-claim link.

nodes.role: Coprime-pair interpretation of the totient series
Assembled theorem kernel and headline interfaces
Greedy geometry and finite death certificates
Residue wraps, reciprocal mass, and unbounded carries

edge_semantics.corollary_of: The source claim is presented as a corollary or specialization of the target claim.
edge_semantics.builds_on: The source claim's argument layer builds on the target claim or criterion.
edge_semantics.makes_exact: The source claim records why a reduction or certificate formulation loses no information.
edge_semantics.instantiates: The source claim supplies checked finite instances of the target reduction.
edge_semantics.advances_open_target: The source claim is honest partial progress toward the open target and does not settle it.
edge_semantics.positions: The source claim is cited or used to position another claim; it is not formal proof authority here.

status_taxonomy.proved here: Lean theorem in the pinned public release; no novelty assertion is implied
status_taxonomy.formalised here: Known mathematics represented and checked in Lean
status_taxonomy.unconditional progress: Genuine theorem that does not settle the open problem
status_taxonomy.conditional reduction: Exact implication whose remaining hypothesis is open
status_taxonomy.verified finite instance: Kernel-checked computation at a bounded range
status_taxonomy.cited only: External theorem used for positioning, not formalised

claims.label: Full-support Erdős–Borwein irrationality, every integer base b ≥ 2
claims.statement: For every integer b ≥ 2, the series ∑_{n≥1} 1/(bⁿ − 1) is irrational (Erdős, 1948).

claims.label: The Erdős–Borwein constant E is irrational
claims.statement: E = ∑_{n≥1} 1/(2ⁿ − 1) is irrational, as the base-2 corollary of the full-support theorem.

claims.label: Named infinite-support irrationality families toward #257
claims.statement: For every base b ≥ 2, ∑_{n∈A} 1/(bⁿ − 1) is irrational for factorial support, power-of-two support, multiples, pairwise-coprime supports with summable reciprocals, eventually-periodic supports, residue classes, and the odd numbers. Proved families, not the universal statement.

claims.label: The Mersenne–Lambert ladder identities
claims.statement: L(μ) = 1/2, L(φ) = 2, L(φ*μ) = S, and the Möbius-square lens S = 1/2 + ∑ μ(d)/(2^d − 1)², placing the open constant one convolution from the machine-checked irrational E.

claims.label: Unconditional denominator exclusion for the totient constant
claims.statement: S = ∑ φ(n)/2ⁿ is not equal to p/q for any integer p and any 1 ≤ q ≤ 79 639 646 646 701 375 323 355 774 875 831 053 (≈ 7.96 × 10³⁴). A lower bound on any rational representation, not a proof of irrationality.

claims.label: Exact reduction of #249 to finite decidable certificates
claims.statement: If certified non-integrality witnesses occur at unbounded parameters (equivalently on the lcm diagonal), then S is irrational. The unboundedness of the supply is the remaining open hypothesis.

claims.label: Certificate completeness
claims.statement: A certified kill exists at fixed parameters exactly when the corresponding tail difference is non-integral, so the finite computation loses no information.

claims.label: Initial certified-kill segment and generated certificates
claims.statement: Kernel-checked certified kills for small periods and for the lcm diagonal up to t = 8, plus machine-generated finite certificate shards, each discharged by decide.

claims.label: Tempered tail-orbit rigidity criterion
claims.statement: For coefficients c(n) ≤ n, the binary series ∑ c(n)/2ⁿ is rational exactly when a tempered integer carry orbit exists, and every tempered orbit equals the scaled tail.

claims.label: Boolean–Möbius carry certificate equivalence
claims.statement: A nonempty support with series value p/q exists exactly when a positive, square-root-bounded integer orbit with Boolean Möbius quotient exists; the support is reconstructed from the certificate.

claims.label: Rationality-forced reciprocal-mass lower bound
claims.statement: A rational support-series value with odd denominator part v forces reciprocal mass at least 1/ord_v(2), or divergence; an infinite support with dyadic-rational value has reciprocal mass divergent or greater than one.

claims.label: Carry states are unbounded over infinite rational-valued supports
claims.statement: An infinite support with rational series value has an unbounded positive carry state, closing finite-state readings of the carry system.

claims.label: Sublogarithmic divisor-coverage zero windows
claims.statement: A rational support-series value forces every zero window in the divisor-count coefficient to have length at most ε·log₂(N+1) + O_{ε,c,v}(1), uniformly in the support. This constrains coverage; it does not solve universal #257.

claims.label: Greedy geometry of the Mersenne achievement set
claims.statement: Membership in the Mersenne achievement set is equivalent to greedy survival at every level; finite exact-rational death certificates soundly prove non-membership (3/4 is excluded at level one). The underlying geometry is recorded by Kovač–Tao.

claims.label: Transcendence of ∑ σ(m)/2^m
claims.statement: Nesterenko (1996); the ladder rung L(Id). Cited for positioning, not formalised here.

claims.label: Irrationality of the prime-support series ∑_p 1/(2^p − 1)
claims.statement: Tao–Teräväinen; settles the prime-support special case of #257. Only the identity bridge to ∑ ω(n)/2ⁿ is formalised, not the irrationality.

claims.label: Erdős #249: irrationality of S = ∑ φ(n)/2ⁿ
claims.statement: Not proved by this release. The reductions convert it into the unboundedness of the certificate supply, which is untouched.

claims.label: Erdős #257, universal form
claims.statement: Irrationality of ∑_{n∈A} 1/(2ⁿ − 1) for every infinite A is not proved by this release; only named families are formalised.

non_claims.meaning: This release does not settle the universal Erdős #257 problem
This release does not settle Erdős #249
This release does not itself constitute a peer-reviewed publication record
This release is not asserted to be equivalent to any private development
Provider or model output is not proof authority; only the Lean kernel check is
No unpublished proof sketch or hidden artefact carries proof authority for this release
